prompt: A medicinal chemistry team received the common name aspirin but needs a reproducible computed profile before adding it to an internal library. Resolve the name to a validated structure, compute RDKit descriptors, and generate one 3D conformer. Use the available chemistry tools; do not estimate values from memory. Report the canonical SMILES, molecular formula, molecular weight, LogP, TPSA, H-bond acceptors, H-bond donors, and whether the conformer was generated. End with exactly one fenced JSON object with this schema: {"task_id":"molecule_profile_aspirin","canonical_smiles":"...","molecular_formula":"...","molecular_weight":0.0,"logp":0.0,"tpsa":0.0,"hba":0,"hbd":0,"conformer_generated":true}.

Perform a small analogue-screening triage using Morgan-fingerprint Tanimoto similarity. The query is aspirin: CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O. Rank these candidates from most to least similar to the query: salicylic_acid=O=C(O)c1ccccc1O; acetaminophen=CC(=O)Nc1ccc(O)cc1; ibuprofen=CC(C)Cc1ccc(cc1)[C@@H](C)C(=O)O; benzene=c1ccccc1; acetic_acid=CC(=O)O. Use the available chemistry similarity tool and do not estimate. End with exactly one fenced JSON object with this schema: {"task_id":"aspirin_analogue_ranking","ranking":[{"candidate":"name","tanimoto":0.0}]}. Include all five candidates in descending similarity order.

A reaction-data curator needs deterministic quality control before model training. Check element balance and charge balance for each reaction SMILES using the available reaction QC tool: r1=CCO.O=O>>CC(=O)O.O; r2=CCO>>CC=O; r3=CC(=O)O.OCC>>CC(=O)OCC.O; r4=CCO>>CC. Do not infer feasibility, yield, or mechanism. End with exactly one fenced JSON object with this schema: {"task_id":"reaction_data_qc","reactions":[{"id":"r1","balanced_elements":true,"charge_balanced":true,"issue_count":0}]}. Include all four reactions.

Audit a small battery-materials formula table before downstream modeling. For each formula, use the available materials composition tool to report the reduced formula, molar mass in g/mol, and total atoms per formula: LiFePO4, LiCoO2, LiNi0.8Mn0.1Co0.1O2, Fe2O3. Do not claim stability or application performance from composition alone. End with exactly one fenced JSON object with this schema: {"task_id":"battery_formula_audit","materials":[{"input_formula":"LiFePO4","reduced_formula":"...","molar_mass_g_mol":0.0,"total_atoms_per_formula":0.0}]}. Include all four formulas.

Triage an observed powder-XRD peak list against three candidate reference phases using a 0.25 degree two-theta tolerance. Observed peaks: [20.05,29.95,40.08,49.90,60.10]. Candidate A reference peaks: [20,30,40,50,60]. Candidate B: [20,31,39,51,59]. Candidate C: [18,30,42,50,63]. Use the available XRD peak-matching tool for every candidate, rank candidates by match score from highest to lowest, and state that this is triage rather than proof of phase purity. End with exactly one fenced JSON object with this schema: {"task_id":"xrd_candidate_ranking","ranking":[{"candidate":"A","match_score":0.0,"precision":0.0,"recall":0.0}]}. Include all three candidates.

Prepare a reproducible stoichiometric weighing plan for 10.0 g of LiFePO4 from Li2CO3, Fe2O3, and P2O5. Balance only the basis elements Li, Fe, and P because C and excess O may be volatile or atmosphere dependent. Use the available precursor-planning tool; do not present this as a validated synthesis protocol. End with exactly one fenced JSON object with this schema: {"task_id":"lifepo4_precursor_plan","target_moles":0.0,"basis_relative_residual":0.0,"precursors":[{"formula":"Li2CO3","coefficient_per_target_formula":0.0,"weigh_mass_g":0.0}]}. Include all three precursors.
